word: G2089 - ἔτι - éti - et'-ee - After that

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἔτι

- Transliteration: Eti

- Phonetic: et'-ee

- Definition:

1. yet, still

a. of time

1. of a thing which went on formerly, whereas now a different state of things exists or has begun to exist

2. of a thing which continues at present 1a

b. even, now

1. with negatives 1a

c. no longer, no more

d. of degree and increase

1. even, yet

2. besides, more, further

- Origin: perhaps akin to G2094

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Adverb

- Strong's: Perhaps akin to G2094; yet still (of time or degree): - after that also ever (any) further (t-) henceforth (more) hereafter (any) longer (any) more (-one) now still yet.
